title: Listening - Part 3: Listening to Podcasts
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they regularly listen to podcasts. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for listening. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they regularly listen to podcasts. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for listening. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about podcasts they listen to regularly.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I studied French at school for years, but when I went to Paris I realised I couldn’t follow ordinary conversation at all. Grammar exercises hadn’t prepared me for the speed people speak. [19]Now I listen to a short French-language podcast on my bus journey each morning so that my ear gets used to natural speech and I can pick up useful expressions.[/19] I don’t understand every word yet, but my listening has already improved noticeably.||Speaker 2 Man: I used to see headlines online and share opinions without really knowing the background. One day a friend challenged something I said, and I couldn’t explain it properly. [20]I started listening to a weekly news podcast because it takes major stories and explains why they matter, rather than just reporting what happened.[/20] It hasn’t made me an expert, but I’m better able to take part in discussions now and less likely to believe the first comment I read.||Speaker 3 Woman: I moved away from home this year and suddenly had to cook for myself. At first I survived on pasta with the same sauce because recipes online seemed complicated and required too many ingredients. [21]A food podcast I found describes simple meals using ordinary ingredients, and I listen each week to decide what I’ll try cooking next.[/21] Yesterday I made a vegetable curry that was actually quite good. My mother is relieved I’m eating something more interesting now.||Speaker 4 Man: I started running because I wanted to be fitter, but I found exercising alone terribly boring. Music helped at first, although after a while I was always skipping the same songs. [22]When I listen to a podcast during a run, it feels as though someone is chatting beside me, and I keep going because I want to hear how the conversation ends.[/22] I now run much farther without constantly checking the time or wishing I were already home.||Speaker 5 Woman: I’ve just started my first office job and some things nobody teaches you, like how to speak in meetings or ask for more responsibility without sounding rude. My colleagues are friendly, but I don’t want to ask endless questions. [23]I follow a careers podcast because people describe situations they faced at work and give practical suggestions that I can use in my own job.[/23] One episode on giving presentations was particularly helpful before I spoke to our team.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I studied French at school for years, but when I went to Paris I realised I couldn’t follow ordinary conversation at all. Grammar exercises hadn’t prepared me for the speed people speak. Now I listen to a short French-language podcast on my bus journey each morning so that my ear gets used to natural speech and I can pick up useful expressions. I don’t understand every word yet, but my listening has already improved noticeably.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: I used to see headlines online and share opinions without really knowing the background. One day a friend challenged something I said, and I couldn’t explain it properly. I started listening to a weekly news podcast because it takes major stories and explains why they matter, rather than just reporting what happened. It hasn’t made me an expert, but I’m better able to take part in discussions now and less likely to believe the first comment I read.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: I moved away from home this year and suddenly had to cook for myself. At first I survived on pasta with the same sauce because recipes online seemed complicated and required too many ingredients. A food podcast I found describes simple meals using ordinary ingredients, and I listen each week to decide what I’ll try cooking next. Yesterday I made a vegetable curry that was actually quite good. My mother is relieved I’m eating something more interesting now.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I started running because I wanted to be fitter, but I found exercising alone terribly boring. Music helped at first, although after a while I was always skipping the same songs. When I listen to a podcast during a run, it feels as though someone is chatting beside me, and I keep going because I want to hear how the conversation ends. I now run much farther without constantly checking the time or wishing I were already home.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I’ve just started my first office job and some things nobody teaches you, like how to speak in meetings or ask for more responsibility without sounding rude. My colleagues are friendly, but I don’t want to ask endless questions. I follow a careers podcast because people describe situations they faced at work and give practical suggestions that I can use in my own job. One episode on giving presentations was particularly helpful before I spoke to our team.

options.text: to learn about a historical topic

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker listens to French podcasts to become better at understanding real spoken French.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker listens to a news programme for a clearer understanding of important current topics.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker uses a food podcast to find manageable new cooking ideas.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker finds that podcasts provide a sense of company when running alone.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker listens for practical guidance on situations encountered in a new job.
